name: Deep-Fried Shrimp Balls with Celery Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 stalk, good-sized celery, very crisp and fresh
recipeInstructions:
1. Devein the shrimp.
2. Chop the shrimp finely, almost into a paste.
3. Mince the ginger and garlic; keep them separate.
4. Peel the tough strings from the celery stock and slice it at an angle into pieces about 1/4-inch thick.
5. In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, oyster sauce, vodka, chicken broth, and 1 tablespoon of cornstarch. Reserve this mixture.
6. In another mixing bowl, combine the shrimp with half of the minced ginger and half of the minced garlic; add the salt and the remaining tablespoon of cornstarch. Mix well and form the mixture into balls about 1-inch in diameter.
7. Heat the oil to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and deep-fry the shrimp balls 6 at a time. Drain the balls on paper towels and keep warm.
8. In a wok, put 1 tablespoon of hot oil from the deep-fry pan and stir-fry the remaining ginger and garlic until it turns golden; add the celery and stir-fry quickly for about half a minute. Add the reserved sauce mixture and stir until it thickens.
9. On a plate, make a bed of lettuce leaves and arrange the shrimp balls on it. Pour the sauce over the shrimp balls and serve hot. Makes 6 to 8 appetizer servings.
